title: T-016 — withScannerPermission HOC + role check (06-a)
prompt:
# T-016 — scanner permission guard

PRD: docs/prd/06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.1 FR-SC-FE-012, §12.5 RBAC
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Deliver `withScannerPermission(workflow)` HOC + `usePermission(workflow)` hook that checks `scanner.access` plus the workflow-specific role (warehouse.receive, production.consume, quality.inspect, etc.). Missing permission ⇒ redirect to /scanner with an amber warn banner. Auth-context comes from session JWT decoded server-side.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/permission-guard.tsx`:
   - `usePermission(workflow: ScannerWorkflow): { allowed: boolean, reason?: string }` from session.roles[].
   - `withScannerPermission(Component, workflow)` returns a wrapper that redirects via `next/navigation` if not allowed and surfaces a query-param 'denied=workflow' for SCN-home banner.
2. Mapping table workflow → required role list in `apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/permission-map.ts`.
3. RTL test verifying redirect + allowed render.

## Files
- apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/permission-guard.tsx [create]
- apps/scanner/src/lib/scanner/permission-map.ts [create]
- apps/scanner/__tests__/permission-guard.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a session with roles=['scanner.access','warehouse.operator'], when usePermission('warehouse.receive') runs, then allowed=true
2. Given a session without 'production.operator', when withScannerPermission wraps a SCN-080 component and that page mounts, then the user is redirected to /scanner?denied=production.consume and the wrapped component never renders
3. Given an empty roles array, when usePermission('quality.inspect') runs, then allowed=false and reason='missing scanner.access'

## Test strategy
- RED: write permission-guard.test.tsx asserting all three ACs before implementation
- Run 'pnpm --filter scanner test permission-guard'

## Risk red lines
- Do not rely solely on client-side guard — every API endpoint must re-check (out of scope here)
- Do not allow guard bypass via unauthenticated layout fallback
- Do not redirect into a loop when /scanner itself is denied

pipeline_inputs.description: RBAC HOC + hook for scanner workflows; redirects unauthorized users back to /scanner with denial banner.
pipeline_inputs.details:
permission-map.ts maps workflow to roles[]; usePermission decodes session.roles and returns { allowed, reason }; withScannerPermission wraps page components and redirects to /scanner?denied=<workflow> when not allowed. RTL test stubs session provider for three role scenarios.

Wave0 scanner parity note: use `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/scanner/flow-receive.jsx:1-260` as prototype reference and include mobile viewport 390x844 screenshot + Playwright trace artifacts in closeout.
out_of_scope: Do not enforce server-side permission here (every endpoint owns its check)
Do not implement SCN-home denial banner UX here (T-023)
Do not implement supervisor override here
Do not implement P2 flows here: transfer-order (TO) creation, replenishment, cycle-count, inquiry full traceability — P1 stubs only
Do not implement offline queue write-path here (P2); P1 keeps offline-safe scan buffer + indicator only
acceptance_criteria: Given the production scanner UI/wiring renders, when compared to `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/scanner/home.jsx:7-61` at mobile viewport 390x844, then structural, visual, and interaction parity is preserved (touch targets >=48dp, scanner dark tokens, badge+PIN auth, scanner.* permissions enforced).
Given roles=['scanner.access','warehouse.operator'], when usePermission('warehouse.receive') runs, then allowed=true

Given roles missing 'production.operator', when a SCN-080 page wrapped by withScannerPermission mounts, then the user is redirected to /scanner?denied=production.consume and the wrapped component never renders

Given empty roles array, when usePermission('quality.inspect') runs, then allowed=false and reason='missing scanner.access' UI closeout includes 390x844 screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: write permission-guard.test.tsx asserting all three ACs before implementation
For UI parity, capture 390x844 screenshots and Playwright trace/video artifacts; attach artifact paths in closeout per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`.
risk_red_lines: Do not rely solely on client-side guard
Do not allow bypass via unauthenticated layout fallback
Do not redirect into a loop when /scanner itself is denied
Do not bypass scanner.* permission namespace when wiring server actions
Do not regress operator-friendly touch targets (>=48dp) or mobile viewport 390x844 evidence
cross_module_dependencies: 02-settings: PIN policy, locale, scanner device defaults
